rerank_alpha.description: CE vs first-stage blend weight (Nogueira & Cho 2019 linear interpolation)
rerank_alpha.conclusion: Monotonically increasing up to 0.70. Higher alpha not tested via official ablation.py.

signal_weights.description: Per-signal weight ablation (one-at-a-time, baseline: fts=0.5, heat=0.3, ngram=0.3)

cross_benchmark_regression.description: BEAM-optimal weights (fts=0, heat=0.7, ngram=0) regress on factual benchmarks
cross_benchmark_regression.conclusion: Corpus-adaptive weights needed. Static weights cannot optimize across conversational (BEAM) and factual (LME/LoCoMo) benchmarks simultaneously.

adaptive_alpha.description: Per-query alpha based on CE score spread (QPP, Shtok et al. TOIS 2012)
adaptive_alpha.approach: Boost alpha above base 0.70 when CE score spread > 0.3, up to max 0.85
adaptive_alpha.conclusion: Adaptive alpha via CE spread does NOT work. Improved LME marginally (+0.003 MRR) but severely regressed LoCoMo (-3.8pp MRR, -5.1pp R@10). CE score spread cannot distinguish 'one great match' from 'one irrelevant outlier'. Fixed alpha=0.70 remains the empirically justified default.

failed_approaches.quality_gating: Count-based signal quality factor in PL/pgSQL — regressed all 3 benchmarks (BEAM 0.511, LME 0.854, LoCoMo 0.790). Reverted.
failed_approaches.pre_retrieval_specificity: Query content word count / ratio / entity presence — features are NOT discriminative between BEAM and LME queries (BEAM ratio=0.470, LME ratio=0.459).
failed_approaches.meta_conversational_detection: Regex patterns for conversation-about-conversation queries — only 20.5% of BEAM matches, 9.8% of LME matches. Not selective enough.
